old_claim.note: Textiles & Weaving provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Chromatographic identification of indigotin is constitutive evidence that the scoped textile was colored with a plant-derived indigo dye.

source_shape.source_locator: HPLC-PDA analysis, Indigo dyes, and Conclusions detect indigotin in Tx1 and identify staining with an indigo dye, most likely woad.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Chemical analysis identifies an indigo colorant in Tx1, while microscopy identifies its fabric structure as single-needle looping rather than weaving.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: A plant-derived dye is required for the scoped color claim, but a weaving dependency would contradict the paper's nalbinding identification.

ontology_before: The dyed-textile node required weaving even though its directly analyzed fabric was not woven.
ontology_after: The scoped nalbinding textile requires the chemically identified natural dye process instead.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: dyed_textiles now requires natural_dyes and no longer directly depends on textiles_weaving.
would_reject_if: Microscopy reclassified Tx1 as woven cloth or chemical analysis withdrew the indigotin identification.
short_reason: Tx1 is indigo-dyed nalbinding, so dyeing is required and weaving is not.
